quoted_tweet.text:
NEWS: Elon Musk retakes the stand on day 3 of OpenAI trial as key legal claims unfold.

Elon Musk's lawyers are building the case around two main claims:

1. Breach of Charitable Trust: They argue Musk's ~$38M donation to OpenAI established a charitable trust, requiring OpenAI to remain nonprofit and open-source forever.

2. Unjust Enrichment: They claim OpenAI, Altman and Brockman used the $38 million for commercial purposes that violated the company's charitable mission, improperly benefiting from Musk's early contributions.

OpenAI's defense: Attorney Savitt told the jury Musk doesn't care about these issues, he cares about "winning." His exact words: "We are here because Mr. Musk didn't get his way at OpenAI."

Microsoft's role: Named as a defendant, accused of aiding OpenAI's alleged breach. Microsoft attorney Russell Cohen says the company had no knowledge of any breach and Musk could have raised concerns directly with CEO Satya Nadella.

quoted_tweet.text:
@techreview Musk says he made the last $5 million quarterly donation to OpenAI in May 2017 and stopped paying for OpenAI's office rent in 2020. 

OpenAI's lawyer points out that Musk's brain implant company Neuralink was part of the same building that OpenAI's office was in and that Musk was on the hook for the lease of the entire building. Musk says he would've sublet the space if he didn't give it to OpenAI. "I gave OpenAI a $3 million a year building," he says.

kept.text:
Neuralink just released a new video on their R1 surgical robot:
• Inserts 128 ultra-thin, flexible threads (each thinner than a human hair, ~1/10th the width) containing a total of 1,024 electrodes directly into living brain tissue
• Thread insertion now takes just 1.5 seconds per thread (major improvement from ~17 seconds previously)
• Real-time high-resolution vision system maps and actively avoids every surface blood vessel to eliminate bleeding risk during insertion
• Enables deeper thread placement (>50 mm), accessing many brain regions
• Redesigned needle cartridge is 95% cheaper to manufacture for high-volume production
• Engineered for compatibility with 99% of the global population
• Full procedure designed for precision, repeatability, and one-day “one-click” automation potential

kept.text:
The Elon Musk effect : Innovator, Job Creator & Humanitarian Visionary

Did you know that Elon Musk’s companies — Tesla, SpaceX, X, xAI, Neuralink, and The Boring Company — employ approximately 150,000 people worldwide?

Tesla alone accounts for around 135,000 of those jobs, powering the future of sustainable energy and transportation.

But Musk’s impact goes far beyond creating employment. He has donated services, products, and time on multiple occasions to benefit humanity:

•  Starlink terminals & free internet service provided to Ukraine (thousands of units during the war), disaster zones hit by hurricanes, and other crises worldwide — keeping people connected when they needed it most.

•  During the COVID-19 pandemic, Tesla produced and donated over 1,200 breathing devices plus tens of thousands of masks to hospitals.

•  Through the Musk Foundation and personal contributions, he has donated billions in Tesla stock to charity and open-sourced Tesla’s electric vehicle patents to accelerate the world’s shift to sustainable energy.

He has also initiated major competitions that directly benefit humanity:

•  Funded the $100 Million XPRIZE for Carbon Removal — the largest incentive prize in history — to fight climate change and inspire global innovation.

•  Launched the Hyperloop Pod Competitions, sparking worldwide student and engineering teams to develop ultra-fast, efficient transportation technologies.

Elon doesn’t just talk about solving humanity’s biggest challenges — he invests his time, resources, companies, and relentless drive to make multiplanetary life, sustainable energy, and a better future for all a reality.

kept.text:
Day 2 - Musk v Altman OpenAI trial

Elon Musk told jurors that Shivon Zilis‘ official’ job title was “chief of staff”.  The projects at Neuralink were being discussed at that time. So her “Chief of Staff” was in reference to Neuralink.

IMPORTANT TO NOTE: 
💥SHITVON ZILIS was being PAID  BY  BOTH -
She was being paid as a “consultant” by @OpenAI as per her deposition AND evidently “Chief of Staff” at @Neuralink by Elon 
- AT THE SAME TIME!!

🚨Whether SHIT ZILIS was called "chief of staff," "executive secretary," or  "office worker," the important thing is that she was the operational  hub. In corporate governance trials, the "office worker" is usually the  person who knows the most about who received what information and when.  If she was the filter, she was the one who decided which of Musk's  emails would be prioritized and which would be ignored.

💥From her emails it seems that she took it as if she could do whatever she wanted. She did whatever she wanted, without consulting anyone, and completely ignoring Musk's instructions.

✅ SHIT ZILIS was an infiltrator for OpenAI or Musk, because she was infiltrated  on both sides, but clearly favoring OpenAI according to the emails.  

✅There are no emails that say she favored Neuralink or Musk, because they  all point to her being against Musk and in favor of OpenAI.
